text:
As 2020 drew to a close, businesses were still dealing with ongoing uncertainty and disruption. Many non-essential businesses in Tier 4 areas of the UK were forced to shut their doors once again, adding pressure to organisations that had already faced an extremely tough year. With COVID-19 continuing to affect daily operations, it became clear that these challenges would extend well into the new year.

The events of the year also pushed businesses to reflect on more than just survival. Cybersecurity, employee mental wellbeing, and workplace culture became key focus areas. The Black Lives Matter movement highlighted the importance of equality and diversity, encouraging many organisations to take a closer look at bias and inclusion within their teams.

At the same time, the way people work has fundamentally changed. Remote working quickly became the norm, while Brexit added another layer of complexity to long-term planning. Taken together, 2020 proved to be one of the most challenging periods businesses have faced in recent history.

To gain deeper insight into how companies have been affected — and what lies ahead — we surveyed hundreds of professionals across various industries, focusing on COVID-19, health and safety, and workplace training. Here's what they shared.

Impact of COVID-19

60.8% said the pandemic has negatively affected their business

45% saw business decline, compared with 22.8% who reported growth

Biggest challenges during the year

Staff absences due to illness or self-isolation

Managing re-opening during and after COVID-19

Staff training

Key concerns now and into 2021

Employee mental health and wellbeing

Health and safety at work

Financial pressures such as budget cuts

Employee Health & Safety

62.8% rely on workplace discussions to stay up-to-date with industry trends

Mental health and building a strong safety culture remain top challenges

91.8% understand their legal responsibilities around compliance and safety

Training importance scored a high 9.3 out of 10

Workplace Training

eLearning is the most common method, used by 67.4%

55.4% renew training only when required rather than on fixed schedules

Quality matters most when choosing a training provider

Cost, consistency, and employee engagement are the biggest obstacles

metadata.notes: English workplace report about COVID-19 impact and workplace training - mixed AI/human version

composition.strategy: Balanced rewrite combining AI and human perspectives
